titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Robinson Smith Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by John Espy

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 12, 2018 at 01:23

did.abstract: Correspondence, writings and miscellanea relating to the issue of the allied nations payment of war debts incurred during World War I, and U.S. tax laws.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles Robinson Smith Papers (MS 455). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of Charles Robinson Smith sent to the Yale Library consist primarily of articles, letters, letters to newspaper editors of Charles Robinson Smith concerning the payment of Allied War Debts. Mr. Smith was a staunch advocate of forgiving the debts - especially those of France. Other material relates primarily to United States Tax laws.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born March 1, 1855 in Buffalo, New York. Died September 7, 1930, in Stockbridge, Massachusetts.

Father, Edwin Smith, a banker; member of firm of Robinson, Smith, & Company, Buffalo; son of James and Sarah (Harmer) Smith, of Wethersfield, Conn. Mother, Jane Ownsend (Mather) Smith; daughter of Elias and Cynthia (Corning) Mather, of Albany, N.Y. Cousins: W. Snowdon Smith, '77S, and his sons, Gurney L. Smith, '14, and N. Jerome Smith, '20.

Buffalo Classical School. Second prize in English composition Sophomore year; dissertation appointment Junior year; first dispute appointment Senior year; member Kappa Sigma Epsilon and Delta Kappa Epsilon. Traveled in Europe 1875-1876; studied law at Columbia 1876-1878 (LL.B. 1878); admitted to practice 1878 and as counselor May 1879; corporation lawyer in New York City 1878-1906 (in partnership with Newell Martin, '75, under name of Smith & Martin from 1890); a founder (in 1899) of General Chemical Company of New York, of which vice-president 1920-1922, general counsel 1921-1922, and associate counsel since 1923; in 1920 helped in merger of that company (which, however, retained its corporate name and existence) with Allied Chemical & Dye Corporation, of which a director since 1922; director of Austen Fox Riggs Foundation, Inc. of Stockbridge (for free treatment of psychoneurotic patients) since 1919; member American and New York State Bar associations, Association of the Bar of the City of New York, and New York County Lawyers Association; awarded Legion of Honor April 1, 1927, for services in writing articles during the war.

Married April 29, 1879, in New York City, Jeannie Porter, daughter of William Porter and Elizabeth Wadsworth (Harris) Steele. Children: Elsa (died in childhood); Gertrude Robinson; and Hilda, the wife of Lyman Beecher Stowe, Harvard '04.
Death, due to myocarditis and arteriosclerosis, occurred at his summer home in 1930. Buried in Stockbridge. By the terms of his will a bequest of $2,500 was made to Yale University.

list.item: TITLES OF SOME OF THE PAPERS NOW IN BOXES 3-5 (NOT IN ORDER)
Smith, C.R. , "America's Debt to the Allies,"
Smith, C.R. , "War Loans and War Services," 1926(2)
Smith, C.R. , "The Ruhr and Treaty Rights," 1923(2)
Smith, C.R. , "Taxation of Stock Dividends…Corporate Surpluses," 1928
Smith, C.R. , "Why We are at War With Germany…," 1917(2)
" The Neech ," "Proposal for a Twentieth Amendment Prohibiting Women," 1923(typescript)
Smith, C.R. , letter to the New York American , 1923
*TYPESCRIPTS OF ARTICLES BY CHARLES ROBINSON SMITH
"Treaty and its Compliance with the Armistice," 1920
"A Very Curious Ancient Law" (children's story), 1925 (and printed copy)
"Geography: Herodotus to Richard Hakluyt and Richard Byrd," 1929
Stowe, Lyman Beecher , "Why Hate the British?"
"Debt of America to the Allies" (2, and manuscript notes, 1 printed copy)
"La Dette de 1'Amerioue envers les Allies" (1, and 1 printed copy)
"N.Y. Women, then First Vote and the War," 1918
"The Reaction of France to a Program of World"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: For an inventory of selected materials in this collectionCollection Inventory.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

persname.part: Knox, S. L. G. (Samuel Lippincott Griswold), 1870-1947
Seligman, Edwin R. A. (Edwin Robert Anderson), 1861-1939

did.unittitle: Typescripts and reprints possibly by Smith
3 scrapbooks of correspondence, typescripts, clippings, and pamphlets, some of which concerns Allied War Debts and post-War France and Germany, and much of which is duplicated elsewhere in collection
